name: requirement_restatement_rejection
description: Pins the REQUIREMENT_RESTATEMENT rejection (engine/validator.py:285,338-347 + _is_requirement_restatement at :634-661) via the Jaccard-overlap branch — not the regex-opener branch. The LLM's narrative reuses 12/14 distinct content tokens from `procedures` after stopword filtering (examine, documented, account, management, plan, confirm, enumerated, types, approved, designated, approval, authority), giving a Jaccard ratio of ~0.857 — comfortably above _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70 at validator.py:127. None of the _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES openers match (narrative starts with 'Examine ', not 'Reviewed [...]; confirmed the requirement', and contains no 'shall ... as required' phrase), so this case isolates the *lexical-paraphrase* detector that the regex set alone would miss. Once restatement fires, classify_narrative returns AMBIGUOUS and STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH also rejects on the same attempt; both classes show up in decision.rejection_log, which is why this case asserts via `rejection_classes_contains` rather than the regex on review_reason (review_reason only echoes the LAST rejection per attempt, which is STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH after both fire). Three identical proposals exhaust the validator loop (max_retries=2 → 3 attempts) because the stub ignores corrective_context — the orchestrator's contract is 'keep retrying until the budget is gone' and this case pins that the budget is actually spent.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization defines and documents the account types permitted within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.

tagged_evidence:
